creator: eric@madrid.lol
text:
I currently use the below server setup.
And it works fine with your default ports

OS:		FreeBSD 11.2-RELEASE amd64
Web Server:	nginx/1.14.0
Server Side:	PHP 7.2.8
Databases:	MongoDB NoSQL 3.6.5
PHP 2 Mongo:	PECL classes for MongoDB 1.4.4

I was just messing around with the "CURRENT" build

OS:		FreeBSD 13.0-CURRENT r339752 GENERIC amd64
Web Server:	nginx/1.15.5
Server Side:	PHP 7.2.11
Databases:	MongoDB NoSQL 3.6.6
PHP 2 Mongo:	PECL classes for MongoDB 1.52

I considered it a bug because previous versions work fine.

Here is the server error:
2018/11/09 01:33:10 [error] 3701#100277: *16 FastCGI sent in stderr: "PHP message: PHP Fatal error:  Uncaught MongoDB\Driver\Exception\AuthenticationException: The SCRAM_SHA_1 authentication mechanism requires libmongoc built with ENABLE_SSL in /usr/local/www/domain.com/file.php:3

If this is the wrong place to post this, please tell me where to go or where I can find help, thank you.

Would be cool to see the MongoDB 4.0.x in the port list. :)

I would like to max out the server I am setting up with:
Server Side:	PHP 7.3.0.r4
Databases:	MongoDB NoSQL 4.0.4
PHP 2 Mongo:	PECL classes for MongoDB 1.53

But the PHP 2 Mongo w/ libmongoc built with ENABLE_SSL would be great.

Thank you for any help you can provide.

Eric.
